case_title: JERRY RODRIGUEZ VS. CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO et al

OPPOSITION TO DEF CITY'S DEMUR TO 2ND AMD COMPLT; P&A FILED BY PLAINTIFF RODRIGUEZ, JERRY

LAW AND MOTION 302, JUDGE MILLER DISCLOSED AT THE HEARING THAT SHE KNOWS AND IS SOCIAL FRIENDS WITH DEPUTY CITY ATTORNEY ELIZABETH SALVESON. JUDGE MILLER PRACTICED LAW IN THE SAME FIRM AS MS SALVESON FROM DECEMBER 1981 - MARCH 1987, AND FROM 1992 THROUGH 1995. THIS WILL NOT AFFECT JUDGE MILLER'S ABILITY TO BE FAIR AND IMPARTIAL IN THIS CASE, AND SHE DOES NOT BELIEVE THAT A PERSON AWARE OF THESE FACTS WOULD REASONABLY HAVE A DOUBT THAT SHE WOULD BE ABLE TO BE IMPARTIAL. THE TENTATIVE RULING WAS READ ON THE RECORD BUT THE COURT DID NOT ADOPT IT. THE COURT CONTINUED DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT TO SEPTEMBER 10, 2013 AT 9:30 AM IN DEPT. 302 FOR HEARING. THE CLERK WILL NOTIFY DEFENDANT'S COUNSEL OF THE NEW HEARING DATE. JUDGE: MARLA J. MILLER; CLERK: GINA GONZALES; COURT REPORTER: ANTHONY C. VAUGHN, CSR # 6185

DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT FILED BY DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO HEARING SET FOR AUG-27-2013 AT 09:30 AM IN DEPT 302

LAW AND MOTION 302, JUDGE MILLER DISCLOSED AT THE HEARING THAT SHE KNOWS AND IS SOCIAL FRIENDS WITH DEPUTY CITY ATTORNEY ELIZABETH SALVESON. JUDGE MILLER PRACTICED LAW IN THE SAME FIRM AS MS SALVESON FROM DECEMBER 1981 - MARCH 1987, AND FROM 1992 THROUGH 1995. THIS WILL NOT AFFECT JUDGE MILLER'S ABILITY TO BE FAIR AND IMPARTIAL IN THIS CASE, AND SHE DOES NOT BELIEVE THAT A PERSON AWARE OF THESE FACTS WOULD REASONABLY HAVE A DOUBT THAT SHE WOULD BE ABLE TO BE IMPARTIAL. THE MATTER WAS CALLED TWICE BECAUSE OF THE LATE APPEARANCE OF MR. RODRIGUEZ. NOTICE WAS NOT GIVEN TO THE CITY AFTER THE TENTATIVE RULING CAME OUT. THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING. DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT IS SUSTAINED, WITH TEN DAYS LEAVE TO AMEND FROM NOTICE OF ORDER TO STATE A CAUSE OF ACTION AGAINST DEFENDANT. OPPOSITION WAS FILED. (PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE A FORM OF ORDER.) JUDGE: MARLA J. MILLER, CLERK: CYNTHIA HERBERT; COURT REPORTER: ANTHONY C. VAUGHN, CSR # 6185 (302)
ORDER CONTINUING HEARING
NOTICE OF ENTRY OF ORDER/NOTICE OF RULING FILED SUSTAINING DEMURRER FILED BY DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO

LAW AND MOTION 302, RULING: BEFORE HEARING, DEFENDANT'S COUNSEL INFORMED THE COURT THAT PLAINTIFF NOTIFIED HIM OF HIS (PLAINTIFF'S) INTENT TO APPEAR AT THE HEARING. THERE BEING NO APPEARANCE BY THE PLAINTIFF, AND NO OBJECTION TO THE TENTATIVE RULING, THE COURT ADOPTS THE TENTATIVE RULING ON DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO'S DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT. THE COURT SUSTAINS THE DEMURRER TO THE RETALIATION (GOV'T 9149.20 ET SEQ.) CAUSE OF ACTION WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND. GOV'T 9149.23(A) PROVIDES: "AN EMPLOYEE MAY NOT DIRECTLY OR INDIRECTLY USE OR ATTEMPT TO USE THE OFFICIAL AUTHORITY OR INFLUENCE OF THE EMPLOYEE FOR THE PURPOSE OF INTIMIDATING, THREATENING, COERCING, COMMANDING, OR ATTEMPTING TO INTIMIDATE, THREATEN, COERCE, OR COMMAND ANY PERSON FOR THE PURPOSE OF INTERFERING WITH THE RIGHT OF THAT PERSON TO DISCLOSE TO A LEGISLATIVE COMMITTEE IMPROPER GOVERNMENTAL ACTIVITIES." (EMPHASIS ADDED). IN THIS CASE, PLAINTIFF HAS NO ALLEGATIONS THAT HE REPORTED MISCONDUCT TO A LEGISLATIVE COMMITTEE. THE COURT SUSTAINS THE DEMURRER TO THE WRONGFUL TERMINATION IN VIOLATION OF PUBLIC POLICY CAUSE OF ACTION WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND. THAT COMMON LAW CAUSE OF ACTION IS NOT COGNIZABLE AGAINST DEFENDANT, A PUBLIC ENTITY. SEE MIKLOSY V. REGENTS OF THE UNIVERSITY OF CALIFORNIA (2008) 44 CAL.4TH 876, 900. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: ERNEST H. GOLDSMITH; CLERK: GINA GONZALES; REPORTER: SHERRY SAWYER, CSR# 5976.

calendar.matters:
CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Jun-09-2014 Off Calendar

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Apr-08-2014 Continued To Jun-25-14 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Mar-11-2014 Sustained
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances:
Andrew M. Gschwind, Esq., Deputy City Attorney, Tel. 415.554.3973, for defendant and moving party;
No other appearance.

RULING: Before hearing, Defendant's counsel informed the court that plaintiff notified him of his (plaintiff's) intent to appear at the hearing. There being no appearance by the plaintiff, and no objection to the tentative ruling, the Court adopts the tentative ruling on DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO's DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT. The Court sustains the demurrer to the retaliation (Gov't 9149.20 et seq.) cause of action without leave to amend. Gov't 9149.23(a) provides: "An employee may not directly or indirectly use or attempt to use the official authority or influence of the employee for the purpose of intimidating, threatening, coercing, commanding, or attempting to intimidate, threaten, coerce, or command any person for the purpose of interfering with the right of that person to disclose to a legislative committee improper governmental activities." (emphasis added). In this case, plaintiff has no allegations that he reported misconduct to a legislative committee. The Court sustains the demurrer to the wrongful termination in violation of public policy cause of action without leave to amend. That common law cause of action is not cognizable against defendant, a public entity. See Miklosy v. Regents of the University of California (2008) 44 Cal.4th 876, 900. Order signed in open court. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith; Clerk: Gina Gonzales; Reporter: Sherry Sawyer, CSR# 5976.

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Feb-03-2014 Continued To Apr-23-14 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Oct-07-2013 Continued To Dec-11-13 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Sep-10-2013 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES - JERRY RODRIGUEZ, PLAINTIFF APPEARING IN PRO PER;
NO OTHER APPEARANCES ON THE MATTER.

RULING - Judge Miller disclosed at the hearing that she knows and is social friends with Deputy City Attorney Elizabeth Salveson. Judge Miller practiced law in the same firm as Ms Salveson from December 1981 - March 1987, and from 1992 through 1995. This will not affect Judge Miller's ability to be fair and impartial in this case, and she does not believe that a person aware of these facts would reasonably have a doubt that she would be able to be impartial. The matter was called twice because of the late appearance of Mr. Rodriguez. Notice was not given to the City after the tentative ruling came out. The Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO's DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT is sustained, with ten days leave to amend from notice of order to state a cause of action against defendant. Opposition was filed. (Prevailing party to prepare a form of order.) Judge: Marla J. Miller, Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Court Reporter: Anthony C. Vaughn, CSR # 6185 =(302/MJM)

DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Aug-27-2013 Continued To Sep-10-13 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 302
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Jerry Rodriquez, plaintiff appearing on his own behalf;
No other appearance.

Judge Miller disclosed at the hearing that she knows and is social friends with Deputy City Attorney Elizabeth Salveson. Judge Miller practiced law in the same firm as Ms Salveson from December 1981 - March 1987, and from 1992 through 1995. This will not affect Judge Miller's ability to be fair and impartial in this case, and she does not believe that a person aware of these facts would reasonably have a doubt that she would be able to be impartial.

RULING: The matter was discussed. The tentative ruling was read on the record but the Court did not adopt it. The Court continued DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT to September 10, 2013 at 9:30 am in Dept. 302 for hearing. The clerk will notify defendant's counsel of the new hearing date. Judge: Marla J. Miller; Clerk: Gina Gonzales; Court Reporter: Anthony C. Vaughn, CSR # 6185
